body: Chris, attached is our proposal to replace the eighteen-year-old roof. Base is $15,000; if the decking needs work once we tear off it could run toward $18,000. Deposit on scheduling, balance on completion. No work until you sign.
snippet: Hey Chris, just checking in on where things stand with the dashboard redesign. D...
subject: Roof replacement proposal - 47 Hillcrest Ave

body: Chris, get me the reconciled income and the deductible side of the teaching and the competition once you and Sienna have trued it up, and keep it to what the filing needs. I will handle the rest.
snippet: Hey Danny, yeah the API issue got sorted out Friday afternoon — integration is c...
subject: Year-end numbers for the filing

body: I moved another $500 into the roof savings this month. We are not where I thought on that bucket, can you have the assistant pull the real number before we call the roofer?
snippet: Chris, putting together the agenda for Thursday's team meeting. So far I have: s...
subject: Roof fund - where do we actually stand

body: Note to self: if the shoulder surgery goes ahead in November it is three months of desk duty and no overtime. Work out what that does to the roof timeline before the Nov 5 consult.
snippet: Good list. I'd add a quick discussion on the new deployment process — there's be...
subject: PT and the surgery timing

body:
Perfect, I'll slot the deployment discussion right after the sprint review. And yes, please send the deck by Wednesday morning so I can include a link in the agenda doc.

Also — are we still swapping tours next weekend? I need to confirm coverage and my lieutenant is starting to ask.
snippet: Perfect, I'll slot the deployment discussion right after the sprint review. And ...
subject: Re: Team meeting Thursday

body:
Hey, remember you have your 1:1 with Mark on Friday. You mentioned wanting to bring up the salary review and the remote work situation. Did you actually write anything down, or are you planning to wing it again 😄

You always say you'll figure it out and then come home frustrated. Let's not do that this time.
snippet: Hey, remember you have your 1:1 with Mark on Friday. You mentioned wanting to br...
subject: Manager 1:1 prep

body: Ha, fair point. I started a list last night — salary review, timeline on the team expansion, and asking about getting a dedicated designer on the product side. Do you think I should bring up the remote policy now or wait until after Q3 results are in?
snippet: Ha, fair point. I started a list last night — salary review, timeline on the tea...
subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep

body: Bring it up now — waiting never helps. Frame it around productivity and output, not personal convenience. You know Mark responds better to data than to preference. Also have a specific ask ready, don't go in vague. What outcome do you actually want — 2 days remote? 3?
snippet: Bring it up now — waiting never helps. Frame it around productivity and output, ...
subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep

body:
Good call. I'm going to ask for 2 days to start, with a 90-day check-in built in. Feels reasonable enough that he won't push back hard. I'll put together a quick one-pager tonight.

Thanks for talking me through this — I'd have gone in rambling without it.
snippet: Good call. I'm going to ask for 2 days to start, with a 90-day check-in built in...
subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep

body: Chris, did you sign up for the mandatory cybersecurity training yet? I saw it on the shared calendar and the deadline is October 11th. HR has apparently been sending reminders to your work email. Please don't let this one slip.
snippet: Chris, did you sign up for the mandatory cybersecurity training yet? I saw it on...
subject: Training reminder

body: I keep meaning to get to it but it keeps getting buried under everything else. How long is the training supposed to be? Every time I try to block time for it something else comes up.
snippet: I keep meaning to get to it but it keeps getting buried under everything else. H...
subject: Re: Training reminder

body: The email said 90 minutes but someone in your team Slack said it's closer to 2 hours if you do the optional modules. You don't have to do those — just the core certification is mandatory. Can you block time this week before the deadline creeps up?
snippet: The email said 90 minutes but someone in your team Slack said it's closer to 2 h...
subject: Re: Training reminder

body: I'll do it Thursday afternoon. I have a lighter day after 3pm and no calls scheduled. Remind me Wednesday night if I haven't mentioned it by then?
snippet: I'll do it Thursday afternoon. I have a lighter day after 3pm and no calls sched...
subject: Re: Training reminder

body: I'll remind you, but also just set a phone alarm right now so you're not fully depending on me for this 😅. The link is in an HR email from last Monday — subject line was something like "Annual Security Awareness Training 2026." Check your work inbox.
snippet: I'll remind you, but also just set a phone alarm right now so you're not fully d...
subject: Re: Training reminder

body: Yo Chris, you free for lunch any day this week? I'm on day shifts Wednesday and Thursday before rotating back to nights. Would be good to actually catch up in person — feels like forever. The cookout doesn't count, you were barely there.
snippet: Yo Chris, you free for lunch any day this week? I'm on day shifts Wednesday and ...
subject: Lunch this week?

body: Thursday works great. I'm remote that day so I can meet anywhere near you, no need to rush back. You thinking the usual spot on Flatbush or somewhere different? Just name the time and I'll be there.
snippet: Thursday works great. I'm remote that day so I can meet anywhere near you, no ne...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body: Hey man, any chance you're free Tuesday morning? I've got nothing going on until noon and it'd be good to actually sit down and talk — not just text back and forth for three weeks straight. Coffee somewhere near the firehouse?
snippet: Hey man, any chance you're free Tuesday morning? I've got nothing going on until...
subject: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Tuesday morning works. I can do 9 or 9:30, just let me know. There's that new place on Myrtle that just opened — supposed to be solid. Otherwise we can do the diner if you want somewhere quieter.

What works better for you?
snippet: Tuesday morning works. I can do 9 or 9:30, just let me know. There's that new pl...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Let's do the new place, I'm sick of the diner. 9:30 works, I'll head over right after morning check-in.

And hey, heads up — I want to talk to you about something. Nothing bad, just been working through some stuff and you're the one person I trust to give it to me straight.
snippet: Let's do the new place, I'm sick of the diner. 9:30 works, I'll head over right ...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body: Dude — Lena's turning 40 in three weeks and I have absolutely no plan. She keeps saying she doesn't want a big deal made of it but you know that's completely not true. Can you and Sienna come if I pull something together? Thinking small group, maybe brunch or dinner.
snippet: Dude — Lena's turning 40 in three weeks and I have absolutely no plan. She keeps...
subject: Birthday brunch plans

body: 100% we're in. Sienna actually asked me about this last week — she had a feeling you'd be scrambling. Brunch is probably the better call for a 40th, feels a bit more special than just dinner. What date are you thinking? I'll check with Sienna tonight.
snippet: 100% we're in. Sienna actually asked me about this last week — she had a feeling...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body: I'm looking at Saturday the 19th. I was thinking that Italian place in Park Slope where we went for your birthday — Gino's? I think they have a private room in the back. Maybe 12 people total. Any idea if they'd do a set menu for a group that size?
snippet: I'm looking at Saturday the 19th. I was thinking that Italian place in Park Slop...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Gino's is perfect, great call. I know one of the managers there so I can probably work out a deal on a set menu. I'll call them tomorrow and get the room locked in.

Sienna can help you think through the guest list if you want a second set of eyes. Lock in the 19th and leave the reservation to me. Lena's going to love it.
snippet: Gino's is perfect, great call. I know one of the managers there so I can probabl...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body: Hey, I got a renewal notice for the streaming bundle — it's jumping to $94/month after the promo ends in August. I also noticed we're still paying for that cloud storage plan we've barely touched in months. Should we shop around or just renew everything as-is?
snippet: Hey, I got a renewal notice for the streaming bundle — it's jumping to $94/month...
subject: Renewal options

body: $94 is too much for what we actually watch. If it's mostly Netflix and the food channel, we can cut this down pretty significantly. Don't renew anything yet — let me look at what we've actually been using over the last few months first.
snippet: $94 is too much for what we actually watch. If it's mostly Netflix and the food ...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: I went back through the last 3 months. We really only use Netflix and Hulu consistently. The sports add-on is technically yours but you haven't logged in since March. I found a bundle deal for $61/month that covers both with no sports package. Should I just switch?
snippet: I went back through the last 3 months. We really only use Netflix and Hulu consi...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: Yeah, go ahead and switch. Cancel the sports add-on too — I can add it back in September if football season actually gets me off the couch. Also cancel the cloud storage, I'll move whatever I need to the free tier. What's the cancellation process like?
snippet: Yeah, go ahead and switch. Cancel the sports add-on too — I can add it back in S...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: Already done. New plan kicks in October 1st, saving us $33/month. Cancelled the cloud storage too — you have 30 days to download anything you want to keep. I think there are some old photo backups from your phone in there, worth a quick look before it closes.
snippet: Already done. New plan kicks in October 1st, saving us $33/month. Cancelled the c...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body: Chris, there's a charge on the joint account from a company called Vertex Solutions LLC — $284, dated March 23rd. I don't recognize it at all. Is this something to do with work or one of your side projects? I don't want to just ignore it in case it's a billing error.
snippet: Chris, there's a charge on the joint account from a company called Vertex Soluti...
subject: Vendor invoice question

body:
That's from work — we use Vertex for API licensing and they occasionally bill through the wrong account. It should have gone to the company card, not ours. I'll follow up with accounting on Monday and get it reimbursed.

No need to worry about it, I've got it handled.
snippet: That's from work — we use Vertex for API licensing and they occasionally bill th...
subject: Re: Vendor invoice question

body: The landlord sent over renewal paperwork. Two options: 12-month at $2,850/month (up $100 from current) or 18-month at $2,780/month. I know we've been talking loosely about buying, but we need to decide by October 15th. What's your instinct?
snippet: The landlord sent over renewal paperwork. Two options: 12-month at $2,850/month ...
subject: Apartment lease renewal

body: The 18-month makes me a little nervous if we're serious about buying — it locks us in longer. But $70 less a month is real money over time. Honestly though, what's your gut? Are we actually in a position to buy within the next 12 months, realistically?
snippet: The 18-month makes me a little nervous if we're serious about buying — it locks ...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body: Honestly, I don't think we'll be ready in 12 months — not with where the market is and where our savings are right now. I'm leaning 18-month. We lock in the lower rate and use the extra time to build the down payment properly. Let's talk tonight and get it signed by end of week.
snippet: Honestly, I don't think we'll be ready in 12 months — not with where the market ...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body: Starting a new weekend thing — I'm going to forward you articles I actually think you'll read instead of letting your tabs pile up unseen. This week: a piece on urban EV infrastructure (feels relevant to your work), a long read on the 2003 Staten Island Ferry crash, and a short one on sleep and productivity you'll definitely ignore.
snippet: Starting a new weekend thing — I'm going to forward you articles I actually thin...
subject: Newsletter — this week's reads

body: Ha — I actually already read the EV infrastructure piece, good find. Sending me the ferry one is manipulative and you know it, I'm going to lose two hours going down that rabbit hole. What's the sleep article? Might as well commit to all three at this point.
snippet: Ha — I actually already read the EV infrastructure piece, good find. Sending me ...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body: The sleep one is from The Atlantic — basically argues that people in deadline-heavy jobs are chronically under-sleeping and overestimating how sharp they are on 6 hours. Forwarding it for no reason in particular. The ferry piece is long but extremely well reported — the section on the mechanical failure is especially good.
snippet: The sleep one is from The Atlantic — basically argues that people in deadline-he...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Read the ferry piece. You were right, it was incredible — I ended up going back and finding all the follow-up reporting afterward. And fine, I'll grudgingly admit 6 hours probably isn't enough. Going to bed earlier this week. Maybe. Don't hold me to it.

Keep sending these — genuinely more useful than anything in my own RSS feed.
snippet: Read the ferry piece. You were right, it was incredible — I ended up going back ...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads
